title: Flutter Frontend: Phase 3 — Core Screens (Read-Only)
description: Build public-facing screens: home, browse with filters, listing detail, static pages. See .claude/plans/2026-02-11-flutter-phase3-core-screens.md

title: Complete Freezed listing models
description: Flesh out Listing, ListingPhoto, ListingUser, ListingFilters Freezed models with all fields matching Django ListingOut schema. Run codegen.

title: Create listings and detail Riverpod providers
description: Browse provider: holds ListingFilters state, calls GET /api/listings/ with query params on filter change, returns AsyncValue<PaginatedListings>, supports pagination. Detail provider: family provider keyed by listing ID, calls GET /api/listings/{id}/, caches result.

title: Build HomeScreen
description: Hero section with Vedgy branding and tagline. Browse Listings CTA navigates to /browse. Post a Listing CTA navigates to /create (auth guard handles redirect). Brief description of Vedgy. No API calls.

title: Build BrowseScreen with filter panel and listing cards
description: Most interactive screen. FilterPanel widget: dropdowns for City, Borough (NYC only), Rental Type, Room Type, Vegan Household, Furnished, Seeking Roommate. Price range text fields with 500ms debounce. Clear All button. ListingCard widget: photo thumbnail, title, price, tags, location, truncated description, posted by. Responsive grid (3/2/1 columns). Loading skeletons, empty state. Pagination via Load More or infinite scroll.

title: Build ListingDetailScreen with photo gallery
description: Full listing view. PhotoGallery widget: horizontal scroll with arrows/thumbnails for multiple photos, single display for one, placeholder for none. All listing fields in labeled sections. Lister info (name, phone if include_phone). About lister, rental requirements, pet policy sections. Permission check: non-active listings show 404 for non-owner/non-staff.

title: Build static pages (about, privacy, contact, terms)
description: Copy content from existing Django templates. Wrap in AppScaffold. No API calls. Wire routes in GoRouter: /about, /privacy, /contact, /terms.

title: Add database indexes on frequently filtered columns
description: The browse page filters on status, city, borough, rental_type, room_type, vegan_household, furnished, seeking_roommate, and price (models.py:29-112). None of these have db_index=True. The status field is queried on nearly every page load. Django adds indexes for ForeignKey and primary_key fields automatically, but not for general CharField or IntegerField columns.

title: Admin approval doesn't check current listing status
description: The admin_approve_listing view (views.py:433-441) can call activate_listing() on ANY listing regardless of current status -- including already active, deactivated, or expired ones. There is no guard like 'if listing.status \!= ListingStatus.PAYMENT_SUBMITTED'. Note: The admin bulk action in admin.py:210-216 does correctly check status before approving. Only the URL-based endpoint is missing the check.

title: Logout uses GET instead of POST (CSRF vulnerability)
description: The logout view (views.py:182-186, urls.py:18) accepts GET requests and immediately logs the user out. This is vulnerable to logout CSRF -- a malicious page can include to log users out. Django's built-in LogoutView requires POST by default (as of Django 5.0). The nav link in base.html:30 also uses a simple tag instead of a form with POST.

title: Add updated_at timestamp to Listing model
description: Listing has created_at but no updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True) (models.py:98). This makes it impossible to tell when a listing was last edited, which matters for sorting by freshness, debugging, and audit trails.

title: Renew button does nothing
description: Deactivated and expired listings in the dashboard (dashboard.html:196, 252) show a 'Renew' button linking to /listing/<id>/?renew=true. However, the listing_detail view (views.py:92-119) never checks for a 'renew' query parameter. Clicking 'Renew' just shows the listing detail page with no renewal logic.

title: Security hardening for launch
description: Checklist of security improvements to address before driving real traffic: 1. Payment flow: Replace ?payment=submitted URL parameter with a proper server-side flow (or at minimum a POST form with CSRF). Currently any draft owner can mark payment as submitted by visiting the URL. Not critical while payments are manual Venmo with admin approval, but should be fixed before scaling. 2. Logout CSRF: Change logout from GET to POST. Currently vulnerable to logout CSRF via . Use Django's built-in LogoutView which requires POST by default. 3. Rate limiting on draft save endpoint: /listings/save-draft/ has no rate limiting. Add @ratelimit decorator similar to auth endpoints. 4. Admin approval status check: admin_approve_listing() should verify listing.status == PAYMENT_SUBMITTED before calling activate_listing(). The bulk admin action already does this check. Consolidated from: vedgyproject-5kh, vedgyproject-1x1, vedgyproject-p2g, vedgyproject-13k

title: Replace print() with proper logging
description: All error output in utils.py uses print() (lines 83, 136, 146, 200, 203, 206, 214, 239, 241). In production (Gunicorn on Railway), these may or may not appear in logs depending on output buffering. The project has a LOGGING config in settings.py (lines 211-237) that goes unused by application code. Should use logger.error() for log levels, timestamps, and structured output.

title: Payment can be faked via URL parameter
description: Any authenticated user who owns a draft listing can navigate to /listing/<id>/?payment=submitted to mark their listing as payment-submitted without paying. This bypasses whatever payment system is intended. There is no payment processor integration, webhook, or server-side payment verification. File: listings/views.py:107-117

title: Templates reference fields that don't exist on the model
description: Several template fields reference non-existent Listing model fields: listing.neighborhood, listing.date_until, listing.size, listing.transportation in listing_detail.html; form.neighborhood.value, form.size.value, form.transportation.value in create_listing.html. These silently render as empty. The create form renders input fields for neighborhood, size, and transportation that users can type data into, but the data is silently discarded on submission because these fields are not in ListingForm.Meta.fields.

title: Listing expiration is never enforced
description: When a listing is approved, activate_listing() sets expires_at = now + 30 days, but nothing ever checks this value. The browse_listings view filters only on status=ACTIVE and never compares expires_at to the current time. Listings remain visible forever. There is no management command, celery task, or cron job to transition listings from ACTIVE to EXPIRED. The EXPIRED status exists in ListingStatus and the dashboard template renders an 'Expired Listings' section, but no listing will ever reach that status.

title: Listing photos not cleaned up on listing delete
description: When a listing is deleted (views.py:337-344), ListingPhoto records are cascade-deleted at the database level, but the actual files (in B2 or on local disk) are never cleaned up. Over time this creates orphaned files that consume storage. The delete_photo view properly calls delete_photo_file(), but delete_listing does not iterate photos first.

title: short_term rental type missing from browse filter
description: The model defines RENTAL_TYPE_CHOICES as sublet, new_lease, month_to_month, and short_term (models.py:33-38). But the browse filter (browse.html:72-75) only offers sublet, new_lease, and month_to_month. Listings with rental_type='short_term' can be created but cannot be filtered for in the browse view.

title: Consolidate dual validation paths (Django forms + Pydantic)
description: Two separate validation systems exist for the same model (forms.py:83-166 ListingForm, schemas.py:9-31 ListingDraftSchema). The Pydantic schema accepts any string for choice fields like rental_type -- it doesn't validate against RENTAL_TYPE_CHOICES. The form's Select widget constrains choices on the client side but the Pydantic path doesn't enforce them server-side. These can drift apart.

title: Flutter Frontend: Phase 2 — Flutter Scaffold
description: Initialize Flutter project with Dio, Freezed models, JWT auth flow, GoRouter, and app shell. See .claude/plans/2026-02-11-flutter-phase2-flutter-scaffold.md

title: Initialize Flutter project in frontend/
description: Run flutter create frontend --platforms web. Clean up boilerplate. Set up analysis_options.yaml with recommended lints. Add to .gitignore as needed.

title: Add frontend commands to Makefile
description: Add: frontend-install (flutter pub get), frontend-run (flutter run -d chrome --web-port 3000), frontend-build (flutter build web), frontend-codegen (dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs), dev (runs backend and frontend in parallel).

title: Add Flutter dependencies to pubspec.yaml
description: Add: flutter_riverpod, riverpod_annotation, dio, go_router, flutter_secure_storage, freezed_annotation, json_annotation. Dev deps: build_runner, freezed, json_serializable, riverpod_generator. Run flutter pub get.

title: Create Freezed data models
description: Create Freezed + json_serializable models: AuthTokens (access, refresh), User (id, email, firstName, lastName, phone, createdAt), LoginRequest, SignupRequest, Listing (stub for now), ListingPhoto (stub), ListingFilters (stub). Set up build.yaml for codegen. Run build_runner.

title: Set up Dio client with auth and error interceptors
description: Create lib/services/api_client.dart: Dio instance as Riverpod provider. Auth interceptor attaches Bearer token from auth provider on every request. On 401: read refresh token from secure storage, call /api/auth/refresh/, retry original request or redirect to login. Error interceptor maps non-2xx to typed exceptions.

title: Create secure storage wrapper
description: Create lib/services/secure_storage.dart: thin wrapper around flutter_secure_storage. Methods: saveRefreshToken, getRefreshToken, clearTokens. Uses localStorage with encryption on web, Keychain/Keystore on mobile later.

title: Implement auth provider with JWT flow
description: Create lib/providers/auth_provider.dart: Riverpod StateNotifier managing AuthState (initial, authenticated, unauthenticated). Methods: init (try refresh on startup), login, signup, logout, refreshToken. Access token in memory only, refresh token in secure storage.

title: Set up GoRouter with auth guards
description: Create lib/router/app_router.dart: GoRouter with redirect logic. Unauthenticated users on protected routes redirect to /login?redirect=<original>. Authenticated users on /login or /signup redirect to /dashboard. Protected routes: /create, /edit/:id, /preview/:id, /pay/:id, /dashboard.

title: Build AppScaffold widget (nav bar + footer)
description: Create lib/widgets/app_scaffold.dart: shared layout wrapping all screens. Nav bar: Vedgy logo, Home, Browse, Post Listing, My Listings links. Conditional auth links (Login/Sign Up vs user greeting + Logout). Footer: quick links, feedback, legal. Responsive: hamburger menu on narrow viewports.

title: Build login and signup screens
description: LoginScreen: email + password fields, Log In button calling authProvider.login(), link to signup and password reset, error display, redirect on success. SignupScreen: email, first name, last name, password, confirm password, client-side validation, Sign Up button, error display for duplicate email/weak password.

title: Dashboard performs in-Python filtering instead of DB queries
description: The dashboard (views.py:231-245) fetches all listings for a user, then creates 5 separate Python lists by filtering status with list comprehensions. The entire queryset is evaluated eagerly (all listings loaded into memory) and iterated 5 times. Django convention is to use separate database queries with .filter() or .values() aggregation.

title: Add pagination to browse and dashboard views
description: Neither the browse page nor the dashboard paginates results (views.py:27-89, views.py:228-257). If there are 500 active listings, all 500 are loaded into a single page. Django provides Paginator for exactly this use case, and HTMX makes infinite scroll or paginated loading straightforward.

title: create_listing.html has template options that don't match model
description: The lister_relationship dropdown in create_listing.html:195-196 includes 'agent' and 'other' options that don't exist in Listing.LISTER_RELATIONSHIP_CHOICES (which only has owner, manager, tenant, roommate). Selecting 'agent' or 'other' would cause a form validation error or silent data loss.

title: Fix template/model sync issues
description:
Templates reference fields and choices that don't exist on the Listing model. This is a single consolidated pass through all templates to fix:

1. Remove references to non-existent fields: listing.neighborhood, listing.date_until, listing.size, listing.transportation (listing_detail.html) and corresponding form inputs in create_listing.html
2. Fix vegan_household browse filter values: template uses 'vegan', 'vegetarian', 'vegan_friendly', 'not_vegan' but model only has 'fully_vegan' and 'mixed_household'
3. Fix furnished browse filter: template uses 'furnished' but model uses 'fully_furnished'
4. Add missing 'short_term' option to rental_type browse filter
5. Remove invalid 'agent' and 'other' options from lister_relationship dropdown in create_listing.html
6. Remove or implement the dead 'Renew' button in dashboard.html (links to ?renew=true which nothing handles)

Consolidated from: vedgyproject-5ye, vedgyproject-ac7, vedgyproject-vzu, vedgyproject-93e, vedgyproject-32k

title: Add model-level validation to Listing
description: The Listing model (models.py:29-112) has no clean() method. No validation that: end_date >= start_date when both are set, price > 0, required fields are populated before status transitions (e.g., a listing shouldn't be activatable without a title and price). The form-level validation in ListingForm helps for browser submissions, but the draft save endpoint bypasses the form and can save invalid data via Pydantic (which has all fields optional).

title: Replace Tailwind CDN with build step
description: The full Tailwind CSS library is loaded from CDN on every page load (~300KB uncompressed) via templates/base.html:13. A production build step with Tailwind's CLI or PostCSS would tree-shake unused classes and reduce this to ~10-20KB typically.

title: delete_photo triggers extra queries - use select_related
description: In delete_photo (views.py:349-368), accessing photo.listing.user causes two additional queries (one for the listing, one for the user). Using get_object_or_404(ListingPhoto.objects.select_related('listing__user'), id=photo_id) would fetch everything in one query.

title: Flutter Frontend: Phase 5 — Polish & Parity
description: Password reset, form validation, error/loading states, responsive layout, production build pipeline. See .claude/plans/2026-02-11-flutter-phase5-polish-parity.md

title: Implement password reset flow in Flutter
description: PasswordResetScreen: email input, calls POST /api/auth/password-reset/. Success message: 'If an account with that email exists, we've sent a reset link.' Actual reset confirm stays as Django template page (email links to Django route). User returns to Flutter app to log in after reset.

title: Add form validation across all forms
description: Client-side: Login (email format, password not empty). Signup (email format, names required, password 8+ chars, confirmation match). Create/Edit (title required on submit, positive price, start < end date, city required). Server-side error display: parse Django Ninja validation errors (loc array) and map to inline field errors.

title: Add loading skeletons and error states to all screens
description: Every data-fetching screen needs: Loading (skeleton placeholders matching layout, not spinners). Error (network error with retry, 404, 403, generic with retry). Empty (no results message with guidance). Apply to: BrowseScreen, ListingDetailScreen, DashboardScreen, CreateListingScreen (on fetch for edit).

title: Make layout responsive across breakpoints
description: Breakpoints: Desktop >1024px, Tablet 768-1024px, Mobile <768px. BrowseScreen: sidebar filters on desktop, collapsible top bar tablet, drawer on mobile. Grid: 3/2/1 columns. ListingDetail: side-by-side desktop, stacked mobile. Forms: two-column desktop, single-column mobile. Nav: full horizontal desktop, hamburger mobile.

title: Add snackbar/toast notification system
description: Riverpod provider for notification state. Snackbars for: listing saved/submitted/deactivated/deleted, photo uploaded/deleted, error messages from failed operations. Replaces Django's message framework.

title: Set up production build pipeline
description: Update railway.json: flutter build web --release, copy build/web/ to backend/staticfiles/flutter/, collectstatic, migrate. Add Django catch-all route serving flutter/index.html for paths not matching /api/ or /admin/. Remove dev CORS origins in production (same-origin). Configure Flutter base API URL: relative /api/ in prod, localhost:8000 in dev via --dart-define.

title: Smoke test deployed Flutter app
description: Verify: Flutter app loads at root URL, /api/docs accessible, client-side routing works on page refresh, auth flow end to end, photo URLs resolve from B2, no CORS issues.

title: Flutter Frontend: Phase 6 — Mobile
description: Extend to iOS and Android: platform-specific adjustments, testing, app store preparation. See .claude/plans/2026-02-11-flutter-phase6-mobile.md

title: Enable iOS and Android platforms
description: Run flutter create . --platforms ios,android in frontend/ to add platform directories. Configure Info.plist (camera, photo library permissions) and AndroidManifest.xml.

title: Platform-specific adjustments
description: Image picker: add camera option on mobile. Navigation: bottom nav bar on mobile instead of top nav, swipe-back on iOS. Deep linking: configure AndroidManifest.xml and Apple Associated Domains. API base URL: absolute https URL for mobile builds. Detect platform with kIsWeb/Platform.

title: Mobile UI polish
description: Touch targets min 48x48dp. Appropriate keyboard types per field. Pull-to-refresh on browse and dashboard. Swipe gestures for photo gallery. SafeArea for notches/home indicators. Status bar color matching theme.

title: Test on iOS and Android simulators
description: iOS: various iPhone sizes (SE, 15, Pro Max) + iPad. Android: various screen sizes and API levels. Verify: token storage (Keychain/EncryptedSharedPreferences), camera + gallery upload, deep links, back button behavior, background/foreground lifecycle (token refresh on resume).

title: App store preparation
description: iOS: app icon 1024x1024, splash screen, bundle ID, version, App Store Connect listing, TestFlight. Android: adaptive icon, splash screen, application ID, signing key, Play Console listing, internal testing track. Shared: privacy policy URL, app description, screenshots.

title: Set up mobile CI/CD
description: Add flutter build apk --release and flutter build ipa --release to build pipeline. Consider Codemagic or GitHub Actions for automated mobile builds. Web build continues via Railway.

title: Flutter Frontend: Phase 4 — Listing Management
description: Build authenticated CRUD flows: create, edit, auto-save, photo upload, dashboard, admin actions. See .claude/plans/2026-02-11-flutter-phase4-listing-management.md

title: Add write endpoints to listings/api.py
description: POST / (create draft, auth required), PATCH /{id}/ (update listing, owner only, partial updates), DELETE /{id}/ (delete listing + photos from B2 + DB, owner only), POST /{id}/deactivate/ (owner only). Add ListingIn schema with all fields optional.

title: Add photo upload/delete endpoints
description: POST /{id}/photos/ (multipart upload, auth required, owner only, validates type/size/max 10, processes via existing utils.py pipeline, returns PhotoOut). DELETE /photos/{photo_id}/ (auth required, listing owner only, deletes from B2 + DB, returns 204).

title: Add admin approve/reject endpoints
description: POST /{id}/approve/ (staff only, calls activate_listing() for 30-day expiration, returns ListingOut). POST /{id}/reject/ (staff only, sets status to DRAFT, returns ListingOut). Verify listing status is PAYMENT_SUBMITTED before allowing approve.

title: Create dashboard and photo Riverpod providers
description: Dashboard provider: calls GET /api/listings/dashboard/, returns grouped listings by status, refreshes on CRUD actions. Photo provider: uploadPhotos (multipart via Dio with progress callbacks), deletePhoto, invalidates listing detail cache after operations.

title: Build DashboardScreen
description: Sections/tabs for each status: Draft, Payment Submitted, Active, Expired, Deactivated. Each card: title, status badge, created date, price, city. Actions by status: Draft (Edit, Delete), Payment Submitted (view only), Active (View, Deactivate), Expired (Edit), Deactivated (Edit, Delete). Empty state per section. Tap title navigates to detail.

title: Build CreateListingScreen with auto-save
description: Multi-section form: basic info, location (borough conditional on NYC), details, living situation, lister info, requirements, photos. Auto-save: POST to create draft on first input, debounced PATCH (2s) for subsequent changes. Visual save indicator. Photo upload with drag-and-drop, preview thumbnails, delete per photo, max 10 enforced. Preview button navigates to /preview/{id}. Consider shared ListingForm widget for reuse with Edit.

title: Build EditListingScreen
description: Same form as Create but pre-populated with existing data. Fetches listing on mount. Same auto-save behavior. Owner-only access via router guard + API check. Extract shared ListingForm widget if not done in Create task.

title: Build PreviewScreen and PayScreen
description: PreviewScreen: read-only view of listing (reuse ListingDetailScreen layout). Submit for Review button navigates to /pay/{id}. PayScreen: matches current pay_listing.html. Submit Payment sets status to payment_submitted via API. Redirect to dashboard with success message. No actual payment integration.

title: Write API tests for Phase 4 write endpoints
description: Test: create/update/delete listing, photo upload (valid/invalid file, too many), photo delete, deactivate, approve/reject (staff only, non-staff 403), owner-only access (other users 403), status transition guards on approve.

title: Flutter Frontend: Phase 1 — API Foundation
description: Stand up the Django Ninja API layer with JWT auth and read-only listing endpoints. See .claude/plans/2026-02-11-flutter-phase1-api-foundation.md

title: Add django-ninja, django-ninja-jwt, django-cors-headers to pyproject.toml
description: Add dependencies: django-ninja, django-ninja-jwt, django-cors-headers. Run uv sync to install.

title: Configure django-cors-headers in settings.py
description: Add corsheaders to INSTALLED_APPS. Add CorsMiddleware before CommonMiddleware. Set CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS to localhost:3000 for dev. Gate production domain behind environment variable.

title: Configure django-ninja-jwt in settings.py
description: Add NINJA_JWT settings: ACCESS_TOKEN_LIFETIME=15min, REFRESH_TOKEN_LIFETIME=7days, AUTH_HEADER_TYPES=Bearer.

title: Create users/api.py with auth endpoints
description: Ninja router with: POST /signup (email, name, passwords → JWT pair, rate limited 5/hr), POST /login (email, password → JWT pair, rate limited 10/hr), POST /refresh (refresh token → new pair), GET /me (returns user profile), POST /password-reset (triggers email, always returns 200).

title: Expand listings/schemas.py with API schemas
description: Create Pydantic schemas: UserOut, PhotoOut (with computed url field), ListingOut (full listing with photos), ListingFilters (query params for browse), PaginatedListings (items + count + page + page_size). Build on existing ListingDraftSchema.

title: Create listings/api.py with read-only endpoints
description: Ninja router with: GET / (browse active listings with filter query params, paginated), GET /{id}/ (single listing with photos, permission check for non-active), GET /dashboard/ (auth required, user's listings grouped by status). Reuse filter logic from browse_listings view.

title: Wire API routers in config/urls.py
description: Create NinjaAPI instance at /api/. Add auth router at /api/auth/ and listings router at /api/listings/. Keep existing template routes unchanged. Verify /api/docs serves Swagger UI.

title: Write API tests for Phase 1 endpoints
description: Test all Phase 1 endpoints: auth flow (signup, login, refresh, me, password-reset), browse with filters, listing detail, dashboard, permission checks (non-owner can't see drafts), rate limiting, CORS headers.

title: Add rate limiting on draft save endpoint
description: The auto-save JavaScript fires every 60 seconds and also on initial page load (after 10 seconds). There is no rate limiting on the /listings/save-draft/ endpoint (views.py:371-417). A malicious client could spam this endpoint to create thousands of draft listings. The auth endpoints are rate-limited (@ratelimit), but this write endpoint is not.

title: HTMX filter hx-include list is manually maintained in 9+ places
description: Every filter element in browse.html (lines 48, 58, 70, 80, 90, 99, 109, 122, 128) repeats the same hx-include attribute listing all filter names. Adding or renaming a filter requires updating this string in 9+ places. A single HTMX form wrapper with hx-include='closest form' or a named form would eliminate this duplication.

title: Wrap multi-step writes in transaction.atomic()
description: create_listing (views.py:264-280) creates a Listing, then creates multiple ListingPhoto records in a loop. If a photo upload fails mid-loop, the listing exists but has fewer photos than expected with no indication of partial failure. Wrapping in transaction.atomic() would ensure all-or-nothing behavior. Similarly, activate_listing() (models.py:100-104) does a read-modify-write without a transaction, which could cause issues under concurrent admin approvals.

title: Browse filter options don't match model choices
description: The browse template (browse.html:97-114) defines its own filter options inline rather than deriving them from the model. Mismatches: Furnished filter uses 'furnished' but model uses 'fully_furnished'. Vegan household filter uses 'vegan', 'vegetarian', 'vegan_friendly', 'not_vegan' but model only has 'fully_vegan' and 'mixed_household'. None of the browse filter options for vegan household match actual model values.
